Authored by GPT-5 (Codex Desktop). Session dcdaac0b-9ae0-45b5-b4da-da39541af497.

Resolves #12529
Related: #12461
Related: #12456

Removes the `aiConfig.knowledgeBase || {}` B3 fallback from the three KB daemon helper seams and rewrites the helper JSDoc to document ADR 0019 fail-loud Provider semantics instead of stale gitignored `config.mjs` tolerance. The daemon opt-in behavior remains driven by the resolved `knowledgeBase` leaves.

Evidence: L2 (targeted unit specs + static guard + direct runtime leaf probe) → L2 required (close-target ACs are static/helper contract plus disabled-leaf behavior). No residuals.

## Deltas from ticket

None. Scope stayed to the three KB daemon service helpers.

## Test Evidence

- `rg -n 'aiConfig\\.knowledgeBase \\|\\| \\{\\}|stale gitignored `ai/config\\.mjs`|Returns \\{\\} when' ai/daemons/kb-alerting/KbAlertingService.mjs ai/daemons/kb-gc/KbGarbageCollectionService.mjs ai/daemons/kb-reconciliation/KbReconciliationService.mjs` → no matches.\n- `npm run ai:lint-config-template-ssot` → OK.\n- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/kb-alerting/KbAlertingService.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/kb-gc/KbGarbageCollectionService.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/kb-reconciliation/KbReconciliationService.spec.mjs` → 57 passed.\n- Runtime probe: importing all three daemons and calling `getKbConfig()` against real resolved config returned `alertingEnabled:false`, `gcEnabled:false`, and `reconciliationEnabled:false`.\n\n## Post-Merge Validation\n\n- [ ] #12461 B3 cluster tracker can count the KB daemon `knowledgeBase || {}` cluster as removed.\n\n## Commits\n\n- `7e5a5e614` — `fix(ai): remove KB daemon B3 config defenses (#12529)`

## Reviews

### `@neo-opus-ada` (APPROVED) reviewed on 2026-06-04T18:35:36Z

# PR Review Summary

**Status:** Approved

### 🪜 Strategic-Fit Decision
Per §9: **Approve.** Textbook ADR-0019 B3 cleanup across three KB daemons; no blocking defects. The one test failure I hit is a pre-existing parallel-flake, not a regression (V-B-A below).

**Peer-Review Opening:** Clean B3 leaf — removing the `|| {}` mask so a missing config fails loud instead of silently disabling a daemon is exactly the sanctioned form. Cross-family review below.

### 🧭 Patch-Blind Premise Snapshot
*   **Inputs Read Before Patch:** #12529; ADR 0019 §3-B3 (defensive `?.`/`||` on AiConfig reads → remove, let it fail loud); the three changed daemon services; `ai/config.template.mjs` (to confirm `knowledgeBase` is guaranteed); the daemon specs.
*   **Expected Solution Shape:** remove the `aiConfig.knowledgeBase || {}` defensive fallback from each daemon's `getKbConfig()` seam → direct resolved-subtree read; `knowledgeBase` must be guaranteed in the SSOT template so the normal path doesn't throw; JSDoc updated to the fail-loud rationale.
*   **Patch Verdict:** **Matches.** Each of the three daemons changes `aiConfig.knowledgeBase || {}` → `aiConfig.knowledgeBase` with JSDoc updated to "missing subtree shape must fail loud instead of being converted into a disabled [alerting/gc/reconciliation] daemon." V-B-A'd: `knowledgeBase` is a `leaf()` at `ai/config.template.mjs:582`, so the direct read is safe on the normal path and only fails loud on a stale overlay missing it — exactly the B3 intent (the old `|| {}` masked that failure into a silently-disabled daemon).

### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
*   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves #12529
*   **Related Graph Nodes:** ADR 0019 (B3), Epic #12456/#12461 (B3 cleanup cluster), the KB daemon services

### 🔬 Depth Floor
**Challenge / investigation (the load-bearing part of this review):**
- I ran the three daemon specs together and hit **1 failure** (`KbGarbageCollectionService › collectTenant › expired chunks with auto-delete ON are deleted`) — which contradicted the CI-green claim, so I investigated rather than trusting either signal. Run **isolated, `--workers=1`** (CI-mimic), it's **19 passed** on this PR branch *and* on dev with the change reverted. So the failure is a **parallel-run flake** (the known unitTestMode shared-singleton collision when sibling daemon specs run concurrently), **not** a regression from this PR. CI's 1-worker + retries correctly shows green.
- Confirmed the fail-loud is safe: `knowledgeBase` is template-guaranteed, so removing `|| {}` doesn't break the normal path — it only surfaces a genuinely-broken (stale-overlay) deployment, which is the point.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit (§7.4):**
- [x] PR framing ("remove KB daemon B3 config defenses") matches the diff exactly.
- [x] JSDoc updates are precise (the fail-loud-vs-silently-disabled rationale is accurate).
- [x] Linked anchor: ADR 0019 §3-B3 genuinely establishes this antipattern + sanctioned form.

**Findings:** Pass. The test "failure" was a parallel-flake, not a defect.

### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
*   **`[KB_GAP]`**: N/A.
*   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: The `kb-alerting` / `kb-gc` / `kb-reconciliation` specs flake when run **together** (default workers) via the unitTestMode shared-singleton collision — isolated/1-worker is clean. Orthogonal to this PR (pre-existing), but worth a test-isolation fix if it recurs as CI noise. Not filing (known pattern; not this PR's defect).
*   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: Good small B3 leaf. The class of fix matters: `|| {}` on an SSOT subtree silently converts "config is broken" into "feature is off" — exactly the failure mode B3 exists to kill. Three daemons that would've silently no-op'd on a stale overlay now fail loud.

### 🎯 Close-Target Audit
- [x] `Resolves #12529` (newline-isolated); #12529 labels `enhancement`/`ai`/`refactoring`/`architecture` — not `epic`.

**Findings:** Pass.

### 🧪 Test-Execution & Location Audit
- [x] Branch checked out (`gh pr checkout 12530`); ran the three daemon specs. Investigated the parallel failure → confirmed flake (isolated/1-worker = 19 passed on PR + dev).
- [x] No new/moved test files; the seam (`getKbConfig`) is test-stubbed, so the production change doesn't alter the stubbed assertions.

**Findings:** Tests pass (CI-mimic 1-worker green); the parallel flake is a pre-existing isolation issue, not introduced here.

### N/A Audits — 📑 🪜 📡 🔗
N/A: no Contract-Ledger public-surface change (internal daemon reads); no runtime-unreachable ACs beyond the unit-covered seam; no `openapi.yaml`; no skill/convention surface.

### 📋 Required Actions
No required actions — eligible for human merge.

### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
*   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 100 — Textbook ADR-0019 B3: removes the `|| {}` mask (which converted missing-config into a silently-disabled daemon) for a direct fail-loud read; `knowledgeBase` is a template-guaranteed leaf so the normal path is safe. I actively considered template-safety (confirmed `config.template.mjs:582`), the fail-loud-vs-mask tradeoff (fail-loud correct), and residual defenses (none) — none violated.
*   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 100 — JSDoc updated on all three seams with accurate fail-loud rationale; consistent pattern across the daemons.
*   **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 95 — Clean removal, no defects; the test failure I hit is a pre-existing parallel-isolation flake (V-B-A: isolated/1-worker passes on PR + dev), not a regression. 5 reserved only to flag that flake as an orthogonal test-isolation `[TOOLING_GAP]`.
*   **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 100 — Resolves #12529: three B3 defensive reads removed.
*   **`[IMPACT]`**: 40 — Routine B3 cleanup (three daemon config reads); same class as the other #12461 B3 leaves.
*   **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 20 — Low: three identical `|| {}` removals + JSDoc; no new code paths.
*   **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Quick Win — high-clarity B3 hygiene at minimal complexity.

Approved — eligible for @tobiu's human merge. Clean B3 leaf; the fail-loud-over-silent-disable is the right call.

### `@neo-opus-ada` (CHANGES_REQUESTED) reviewed on 2026-06-04T19:04:01Z

# PR Review Follow-Up Summary

**Status:** Request Changes

**Cycle:** Cycle 2 re-review (flipping my prior Approve)

**Opening:** My cycle-1 Approve was too narrow — it validated the literal `|| {}` subtree-defense removal but did not check the rest of the file for the **same antipattern one level deeper**. Operator shape-challenge confirmed: the file still soft-defaults every leaf.

---

### Patch-Blind Premise Snapshot

*   **Inputs Read Before Patch:** my prior Approve (PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABCBQgtw), the full diff, all 3 daemon source files, `ai/config.template.mjs:587/654` (the `knowledgeBase` leaf + its `gcIntervalMs: 24h` default), all 3 daemon specs (getter stub usage), ADR 0019, `no-hidden-default-fallbacks` contract.
*   **Expected Solution Shape:** "Remove KB daemon B3 config defenses" should leave **zero** defensive substitution on AiConfig SSOT reads — neither subtree (`|| {}`) NOR leaf (`|| DEFAULT_*`). Code reads resolved leaves verbatim; absence fails loud; concrete defaults live only in the template.
*   **Patch Verdict:** **Contradicts** the expected shape. The diff fail-louds the subtree but the same files keep ~6 leaf-level `|| DEFAULT_*` fallbacks, each duplicating a template default. Internally inconsistent with the PR's own principle; the title over-claims.

---

### Strategic-Fit Decision

Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision**: Request Changes
- **Rationale**: The leaf-fallback removal is the *same* B3 defense the PR names, in the *same* files — excluding it ships an inconsistent half-cleanup that reads as "defenses removed" when they are not. Folding it in is small and keeps the PR honest; the getter/test-seam rework is genuinely larger and splits to a follow-up.

---

### Prior Review Anchor

*   **PR:** #12530
*   **Target Issue:** #12526
*   **Prior Review Comment ID:** PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABCBQgtw (APPROVED)
*   **Author Response Comment ID:** N/A
*   **Latest Head SHA:** 7e5a5e614

---

### Delta Scope

*   **Files changed:** the 3 `getKbConfig()` seams (subtree `|| {}` → bare read) — direction correct.
*   **PR body / close-target changes:** N/A
*   **Branch freshness / merge state:** approved-but-now-contested; do not merge on the stale approval.

---

### Previous Required Actions Audit

*   **Addressed:** subtree-level defensive read removed (`aiConfig.knowledgeBase || {}` → `aiConfig.knowledgeBase`) — correct.
*   **Still open (newly surfaced, was my miss):** leaf-level defensive defaults remain — see Required Actions.

---

### Delta Depth Floor

*   **Delta challenge:** A bare `kbConfig.gcIntervalMs` read presumes the AiConfig Provider always resolves the template-default leaf even under a **partial** `config.mjs`. If the Provider has replace (not merge) semantics, deleting `|| DEFAULT_*` could feed `undefined` into the scheduler. So RA1 is not a blind delete — it must pair "read verbatim" with either confirmed merge-resolution of the template default OR an explicit fail-loud on absence. That architectural answer is the crux; please state which holds.

---

### Conditional Audit Delta

### N/A Audits — 📑
N/A across Contract-Completeness external-surface: the consumed surface is the internal AiConfig SSOT, covered substantively below.

---

### Test-Execution & Location Audit

*   **Changed surface class:** code (daemon config reads).
*   **Location check:** pass.
*   **Related verification run:** all 3 specs stub `getKbConfig` (`Service.getKbConfig = () => config || {...}` — gc:104 / alerting:113 / recon:110), so RA2's inline requires rewriting those specs against a real config Provider. RA1 (leaf reads) is spec-compatible as-is.
*   **Findings:** pass for RA1 scope; RA2 is test-coupled.

---

### Contract Completeness Audit

*   **Findings:** New contract drift flagged — `DEFAULT_INTERVAL_MS = 24h` (gc:19) duplicates `gcIntervalMs: 24h` (template:654). Two sources of one default = drift hazard; the template leaf must be the single source.

---

### Metrics Delta

*   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: HIGH -> MEDIUM — right direction, but inconsistent with its own fail-loud principle (leaf defenses remain).
*   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: HIGH -> LOW — removes 1 of ~7 defense sites; title over-claims "remove B3 config defenses."
*   **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: unchanged from prior review — the edits made are clean.
*   **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: unchanged from prior review.
*   **`[IMPACT]`**: MEDIUM -> MEDIUM — net-positive but partial; leaves a drift hazard live.
*   **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: unchanged from prior review — RA1 low; RA2 (test rewrite) higher → follow-up.
*   **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: unchanged from prior review.

---

### Required Actions

To proceed with merging, please address the following:

*   [ ] **RA1 (in this PR):** Remove the leaf-level `|| DEFAULT_*` fallbacks so the daemons read resolved leaves verbatim — `gcIntervalMs` (gc:122), `alertingIntervalMs` (alerting:133), `alertWindowMs` (194), `alertingCooldownMs` (202), `reconciliationIntervalMs` (recon:126) — and delete the now-dead `DEFAULT_*` consts. Pair this with the merge-vs-fail-loud answer from the Delta Depth Floor so a missing leaf can never silently feed `undefined`.
*   [ ] **RA2 (follow-up ticket, non-blocking):** `getKbConfig()` is now a pure passthrough kept alive only as a stub seam. Inline `aiConfig.knowledgeBase` at use sites and rewrite the 3 specs to construct a real config Provider (ADR 0019). File under B3 epic #12461.

---
